subject: Add SKLazy node and lazy forcing to SK reducer
description: Add an SKLazy node type that wraps a Ruby thunk (Proc). When the reducer encounters an SKLazy in a position where it needs a value (e.g., argument to a strict primitive, head of a cons being printed), it forces the thunk and continues reducing. This is the foundation for lazy stream IO — the input stream is a cons list whose elements are SKLazy thunks that read from stdin on demand.
